source: Trimorphic Protennoia (NHC XIII,1), trans. John D. Turner, in The Nag Hammadi Library in English, ed. James M. Robinson (HarperCollins, 1990). File: Research/the nag hammadi library.pdf — pages 527-538.
cross_references: Turner, J.D. (2001). Sethian Gnosticism and the Platonic Tradition. Peeters/Laval.
Turner, J.D. (2007). 'The Sethian School of Gnostic Thought' in The Nag Hammadi Scriptures, ed. Marvin Meyer (HarperOne).
Logan, A.H.B. (1996). Gnostic Truth and Christian Heresy. T&T Clark.
King, K.L. (2006). The Secret Revelation of John. Harvard University Press.

nodes.name: The Invisible Spirit (Father)
nodes.notes: Protennoia identifies herself as 'the Image of the Invisible Spirit' — the Invisible Spirit is above her, the absolute transcendent Father. 'The [immeasurable] Invisible One who is hidden.' Structurally identical to the Apocryphon's Invisible Spirit/Monad. This confirms the shared Sethian cosmological framework.

nodes.name: Protennoia (Barbelo / First Thought)
nodes.notes: The most distinctive node in the Sethian corpus. In the Apocryphon of John, Barbelo is a straightforward first emanation. Here, Protennoia IS Barbelo and is explicitly described as triadic: 'I am the Voice... I am the Speech... I am the Word.' The three modes are not separate nodes (they are one entity) but encode three modes of cosmic activity: giving form, giving life, and giving salvation. COMPARE: Poimandres' Logos is a separate emanation from the divine; Trimorphic Protennoia's Logos/Word is the third mode of the FIRST emanation itself. See encoding_decisions Entry 18.

nodes.name: Autogenes (Perfect Son / Christ)
nodes.notes: 'The Perfect Son revealed himself to his Aeons who originated through him.' Identical structural position to the Apocryphon's Autogenes. He establishes the Four Luminaries and their aeons. Called 'the only-begotten God' — directly created by the Father through Protennoia.

nodes.name: Four Luminaries (Armozel, Oroiael, Daveithai, Eleleth)
nodes.notes: The four great aeons, each with three sub-aeons (12 total). Established by Autogenes. CRITICAL DIFFERENCE FROM APOCRYPHON: In the Trimorphic Protennoia, Yaltabaoth arises when Eleleth (the fourth luminary) speaks — not directly from Sophia's fall. 'Then there came forth a word from the great Light Eleleth and said, I am King! ...there appeared the great Demon.' Sophia/Epinoia is already present in the scene — Yaltabaoth snatches power FROM her rather than being born FROM her. See encoding_decisions Entry 19.

nodes.name: Sophia / Epinoia (Fallen Light)
nodes.notes: Called 'Epinoia' (inner reflection/afterthought) in TP rather than Sophia (wisdom) as in the Apocryphon. She is 'the innocent one' from whom Yaltabaoth 'snatched power' — she is the victim of the Demiurge's usurpation rather than the mother of his birth. After Yaltabaoth appears, she appeals for 'another order so that you may become for me a dwelling place' — she accepts a diminished position. The `fallen` functional role is preserved: she is the aeon who exists in the transitional space between ordered Pleroma (above) and disordered creation (below). Edge from Four Luminaries to Sophia typed `fragmentation` — she descends from her Pleromatic station.

nodes.name: Yaltabaoth (Saklas / Samael)
nodes.notes: Same character as in the Apocryphon — ignorant, arrogant, malicious. 'He has neither form nor perfection.' He 'produced aeons in the likeness of the real Aeons, except that he produced them out of his own power.' He 'produced a man in my [Protennoia's] likeness' — material humanity. The triple name (Yaltabaoth/Saklas/Samael) is identical to the Apocryphon. Edge from Sophia to Yaltabaoth is typed `fragmentation` — in TP he arises through Eleleth's word but derives his existence from usurping Sophia's power.

nodes.name: Archons (Powers of the Underworld)
nodes.notes: 'The great Demon began to produce aeons in the likeness of the real Aeons.' The Archons govern the material cosmos. Identical structural role to the Apocryphon's Archons.

nodes.name: Material World (Chaos / Underworld)
nodes.notes: 'The Archigenetor of ignorance reigned over Chaos and the underworld and produced a man in my likeness.' The material cosmos and material humanity. Same structural position as Apocryphon's Material World.

edges.notes: The Invisible Spirit's own thought becomes Protennoia/Barbelo. 'I am the Image of the Invisible Spirit and it is through me that the All took shape.' Natural self-knowing overflow of the transcendent.
'The Perfect Son revealed himself to his Aeons who originated through him.' Autogenes emerges through and from Protennoia.
'The first Aeon, he established over the first: Armozel...' Autogenes establishes the Four Luminaries and their constituent aeons.

Sophia/Epinoia descends from her Pleromatic station. In TP, this is catalyzed by Eleleth's proclamation ('I am King!') which creates a rupture at the boundary of the Pleroma. The 'innocent one' descends into the lower region.

Yaltabaoth arises by 'snatching power from the innocent one (Sophia) who had descended.' The mechanism differs from the Apocryphon (where Sophia gives birth to him) but the structural relationship (Sophia → Yaltabaoth via fragmentation) is the same. Sophia is the source of his power even if not his literal birth-mother.
'The great Demon began to produce aeons in the likeness of the real Aeons, except that he produced them out of his own power.' Deliberate imitative creation.
The Archons govern Chaos and the Underworld; Yaltabaoth (Archigenetor) produces material humanity. The material world is the endpoint of the cosmic descent.

topology.distinctive_feature: Triadic first emanation (Protennoia as Voice/Speech/Word) and Sophia as victim/usurped rather than unauthorized actor

shared_features: Same Four Luminaries (Armozel, Oroiael, Daveithai, Eleleth) at level 3
Same Yaltabaoth (Saklas/Samael) as demiurge at level 5
Same Archons as intermediary at level 6
Same edge-type pattern: emanation (0-3) → fragmentation (3-5) → creation (5-7)
key_differences: Protennoia = Barbelo explicitly (unified, not separate layers)
Protennoia is triadic (Voice/Speech/Word) — no equivalent in Apocryphon
Adamas (Perfect Man) is absent as a separate structural node
Sophia is 'usurped' by Yaltabaoth rather than giving birth to him — different etiology, same structure
Node count: 8 (vs. Apocryphon's 9) — Adamas omission accounts for the difference

with_adamas.description: 9-node encoding adding Adamas (Perfect Man) between Luminaries and Sophia
with_adamas.rationale: The Apocryphon has Adamas as a separate structural level; TP doesn't explicitly include him but the Sethian framework implies his presence.
with_adamas.effect: Would make the schema structurally identical to the Apocryphon — analytically less interesting.

encoding_notes: The Trimorphic Protennoia and the Apocryphon of John share the same Sethian cosmological framework. The topological difference is minimal (8 vs. 9 nodes, Adamas omitted). The meaningful difference is semantic: Protennoia's triadic identity and the different causation of Sophia's situation. The cross-traditional paper should note the high similarity between these two traditions (same topology family, same edge-type pattern) as evidence of shared cosmological structure within the Sethian school.
